Physical Address: Institute of Applied Chemistry, Berlin-Adlershof e.V. (ACA) Rudower Chaussee 5 D-12484 Berlin, Germany
Critical Condition Basis: Liquid Adsorption Chromatography at Critical Conditions (LACCC)
Evidence Text: Liquid Adsorption Chromatography at Critical Conditions (LACCC) of polymers in combination with Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption / Ionization Time-Of-Flight Mass Spectrometry (MALDI-TOF-MS) and different types of detectors is a very convenient method for the characterization of polymer heterogeneities.

Evidence Text: Liquid Adsorption Chromatography at Critical Conditions (LACCC) of polymers in combination with Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption / Ionization Time-Of-Flight Mass Spectrometry (MALDI-TOF-MS) and different types of detectors is a very convenient method for the characterization of polymer heterogeneities.

Physical Address: Institute of Applied Chemistry, Berlin-Adlershof e. V. (ACA), Rudower Chaussee 5, D-12484 Berlin, Germany
Evidence Text: The 'critical' conditions for poly-1-lactide were realised by a mixture of 60vol.% dioxane and 40vol.% n-hexane which was used as solvent and mobile phase. The column was thermostated at 30°C.
Notes: LACCC analysis performed on oligo-L-lactide using critical conditions established for poly-L-lactide.

Physical Address: Technical University of Berlin, Institute of Technical Chemistry, Sekr. TC 3, Str. d. 17. Juni 135, D-10623 Berlin, Germany; Institute of Applied Chemistry, Berlin-Adlershof e. V. (ACA), Rudower Chaussee 5, D-12484 Berlin, Germany
Evidence Text: The 'critical' conditions for poly-1-lactide were realised by a mixture of 60vol.% dioxane and 40vol.% n-hexane which was used as solvent and mobile phase. A Nucleosil 300-S column (250mm 4mm ID, Macharey-Nagel, Diuren, Germany) was used. The column was thermostated at 30°C.
Notes: LACCC analysis performed on oligo-L-lactide using critical conditions established for poly-L-lactide.

Physical Address: Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland
Critical Condition Basis: elution volumes showed no dependence on molar mass
Detector: Waters 486 tunable UV detector at 235 nm
Evidence Text: At the critical point of adsorption, a series of linear C4H9-PLA-OH's having molar masses (Mn) in the range from 2.3 × 10³ to 7.4 × 10⁴ ... showed no dependence of the elution volumes on molar mass. ... LC-CC conditions have been used corresponding to silica gel as the stationary phase and a mixture of 1,4-dioxane/n-hexane (56.25/43.75 by vol%) at 50 °C as the mobile phase.
Notes: Critical conditions established for linear PLA used to analyze star-shaped PLA.

Physical Address: Deutsches Kunststoff-Institut (German Institute for Polymers), Schlossgartenstr. 6, 64289 Darmstadt, Germany
Critical Condition Basis: critical conditions determined for linear PLAs
Evidence Text: The critical conditions for linear PLAs with molar masses ranging from 4800 to 26000 g/mol were determined at 50 8C at a composition of 57.1/42.9 (1,4-dioxane/nhexane). Despite the different molar masses, all samples elute at the same elution volume (Fig. 1).
Notes: Columns connected in series. Used to analyze star-shaped PLAs.
